summary: Iteration 50 regression suite for the teaching_load orphan-filter + cleanup-orphans endpoint + startup dedup migration. 9 new pytest cases in test_teaching_load_orphan_dedup.py all PASS (1 was initially soft-failing on an over-strict count assertion → relaxed to ≤ baseline; root cause documented below). Prior 14 regression tests (test_teaching_load_bulk_semester_fix.py + test_teaching_load_semester_backfill.py) still PASS — total 23/23.

test_results.Final cleanup: PASS (loads restored, courses removed, inactivated courses re-activated)

critical_code_review_comments: teaching_load.py is still ~2.1k lines — splitting into list/export/cleanup/dedup submodules would improve maintainability (carried over from iter 49).

cleanup_orphan_teaching_loads (and the startup variant) iterate per-load and call find_one per course (N+1 query). Fine at current size (~9 loads) but should batch via $lookup or a $in fetch + map for >10k loads.

Dedup keeps the largest _id (newest) but discards updated_at history of the older record. Acceptable, but worth logging the deleted ids in activity_log for audit trail (currently only the startup variant logs an aggregated count).
seed_data_creation: Per-test ephemeral teaching_loads + courses prefixed 'TLORPH_'. test_zz_cleanup removes everything (3 cleanup paths: tracked ids, inactivated-real-course restore to is_active=True, prefix sweep). Verified DB returns to original state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All three layers of the orphan/dedup fix are now covered. If extending: (a) add a startup-restart simulation by invoking cleanup_orphan_teaching_loads_internal() + dedup_teaching_loads_internal() back-to-back to verify ordering is safe; (b) extend Step 6 to also assert weekly_hours of the kept record matches the newer record (currently only asserts _id match); (c) add a multi-teacher dedup case to ensure groups isolate by teacher_id correctly. The test file is fully idempotent and re-runnable.
rca of the issue: First run of test_orphan_loads_hidden_from_list failed because the assertion expected the active-listing count to stay AT baseline after seeding 2 orphans. The seed step flips an existing active course to is_active=False — and that course happened to already be referenced by a legit pre-existing teaching_load. So the same orphan-filter that hides our two seeds ALSO correctly hides that legit load, dropping the count by 1. The fix was conceptual (assertion was wrong, not the code): relaxed to len(items) ≤ baseline + explicit check that seeded ids are absent. Backend behavior is correct.
